title: ++CHRCH Access
section: THE BOOK OF DARES: Life Is Art
body_html:
5. Drive-By For Beginners (with Natalie)

But it's what happened AFTER the shoot that's the most amazing part to me...

Complete and Continue

                      Got lots of respect for Natalie.....I love the spontaneity of being able to get out of yourself, your head, your ego, your self-criticisms, and be in the moment with a complete stranger. I’m not a photographer but I’ve experienced similar “drive-by” activity for work, and even in church activities as a teen. This has me thinking about what that might look like with my poster art.

                                wow. This is so cool. I have goosebumps. First off, I love what she captures in her photos. She has a knack for capturing the moment when the person really looks into the camera and doesn't "see" the camera. It's like the person is looking at ME (the viewer) - it's awesome. She is really talented. Second, I love seeing her process of going through the nervousness and then doing it. I love the coaching. Telling her exactly what to look for and what to do. Golden. Thank you.

                                        Gavin Wren
                                        4 years ago
                                        Reply
                                        Link
                                        Trying to think of a way to adapt this. I worked as a food photographer for several years and did projects in the UK and latin America which required asking to take photos of strangers, their restaurants, shops etc but frankly I still never got comfortable with it. I originally practiced by walking up and down Oxford Street in London asking to take photos of people and doing the same at street food markets. Even though I've done it many, many times it still freaks me out slightly. Now my focus is creating video content for social media. Wondering whether to photo strangers again, simply because I know it's an uncomfortable exercise, or whether to add a different dimension such as interview, but to be honest, interviewing people is easier than photos-only.

                                            PLAY YOUR MUSIC FOR STRANGERS CASEY! Listen to the keywords they use. Get a feel for what moves them about it; the sound? the lyrics? the drums? And use everything you learn in your marketing tools. Everything in The Book Of Dares is to help you prepare you for when the career defining moment is in front of you... And you'll be like "I got this".

                                                Leo Fialho
                                                5 years ago
                                                Reply
                                                Link
                                                This is very much like what we call “cold-scouting” in the Location Scouting business, where you walk up to someone’s house or business and ask them if you can photograph their property for consideration on a production. It’s nerve-wracking to start, and even though I have 17+ years experience I still get the anxiety when I start a cold-scouting day, but then you just do it once, get the ball rolling, and it all flows from there.
